properties.name: classNames
properties.comment: additional classnames the ui should add to the root of this data point

properties.name: description
properties.comment: description of this data point

properties.name: disabled
properties.comment: if changes to this data point are disabled

properties.name: placeholder
properties.comment: if supported, will add a placeholder for the input element

properties.name: hidden
properties.comment: if this data point should be hidden from display

properties.name: title
properties.comment: title of this data point

properties.name: readOnly
properties.comment: disable edit of form, but allow selection and copy of value

properties.name: required
properties.comment: flag data as required (visual only)

properties.name: canAddItem
properties.comment: arrays only: if an item may be added without causing a validation error

properties.name: canRemoveItem
properties.comment: arrays only: if an item may be removed without causing a validation error

properties.text: onEvent: (root: JsonNode, event: PluginEvent) => void | [JsonNode, Change[]];
properties.name: onEvent
properties.comment:
Called for all events, e.g. `create`, `update`, `delete`, `undo`, etc.
Each event loop is finished by a done-event, which contains a list of modified nodes

`onEvent` may return a new root-node along with a list of changes

properties.name: onDestroy
properties.comment: Called when Editor before Editor is going to be destroyed

typeParameters.text: Signature extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>
typeParameters.name: Signature

properties.name: schema
properties.comment: The JSON Schema describing the form and its resulting data. JSON Schema `type` keywords are required for json-editor to work correctly.

properties.name: data
properties.comment: The initial, partial data to be used to fill the form with values. The data has to match the JSON Schema or it will be ignored or invalidated in the we form

properties.name: validate
properties.comment: if data should be initially validated

properties.name: addOptionalProps
properties.comment: if all optional properties should be added when missing

properties.name: extendDefaults
properties.comment: if json-schema default-values should be extended (required properties, min-length of items). Defaults to false

properties.name: removeInvalidData
properties.comment: Remove data that does not match input schema. Defaults to false

properties.name: options
properties.comment: input options for this editor instance

properties.name: plugins
properties.comment: list of active plugins

properties.name: root
properties.comment: Editor root node

properties.name: schemaNode
properties.comment: JSON Schema API

properties.name: schemaNodeConfig
properties.comment: JSON Schema API options

methods.name: addPlugin

parameters.name: pointer
parameters.comment: A JSON Pointer string to the value to add

methods.name: addValue

tags.comment: addValue **only supports adding properties**

methods.comment: Add a value at a given location. This usually is used to add array-items

parameters.name: node
parameters.comment: ArrayNode of item

parameters.name: itemSchema
parameters.comment: JSON Schema of the item to add

methods.name: appendItem
methods.comment: Add an item defined by a JSON Schema to the end of the target array

methods.name: destroy

tags.comment: plugins will receive an onDestroy call once and are responsible to release memory on their own

methods.comment: destroys editor instance including all plugins, releasing used memory.

methods.name: findPlugin
methods.comment: Get a plugin by pluginId

returns.name: SchemaNode
returns.comment: List of available JSON subschemas to insert into the array. Access the JSON Schema of each option by `SchemaNode.schema`. For more details see [json-schema-library#schemanode-methods](https://github.com/sagold/json-schema-library#schemanode-methods)

methods.name: getArrayAddOptions
methods.comment: Get all available items that can be added next to the specified array

parameters.name: pointer
parameters.comment: A JSON Pointer string of the data to return. Use `undefined` or `""` to get all data or specify a path like `/header` or `/todo/4` to get the data from the requested location

methods.name: getData
methods.comment: Returns the current json data

returns.comment: Current list of form validation errors of the current state.

methods.name: getErrors
methods.comment:
Get current validation errors of node-tree.
An error contains the following properties
`{ type: "error", code: string, message: string, data }`.
The property `data` contains details to the error.
For further details check [json-schema-library#validate](https://github.com/sagold/json-schema-library#validate)

Editor.name: Editor

tags.comment:
import { Editor } from '@sagold/react-json-editor';

const editor = new Editor(options);
Each JSON Schema requires a valid JSON Schema type keyword

Editor.comment: `new Editor` returns your editor-instance containing a JSON Schema tree, registered widgets and plugins and the overall state of the data.

properties.name: onChange
properties.comment:
A function called after each update which that accepts three arguments:

- `data` the latest data of the web form
- `root` the rootNode of the json-editor and
- `editor` the current editor instance

_Only supported by useEditor_

properties.name: widgets
properties.comment:
The list of available widgets to render the JSON Schema tree. Each node in the tree should
have a matching widget to be rendered or the editor will either ignore or show an error
using an Error Widget if added.

properties.name: cacheKey
properties.comment:
A unique id that if changed, will recreate the editor

_Only supported by useEditor_

properties.name: addOptionalProps
properties.comment: if all optional properties should be added when missing

properties.name: liveUpdate
properties.comment: set tpo true to validate while typing. Defaults to onBlur

returns.comment: Returns an instance of `Editor`. For details on working with `editor` see Usage below or more in depth details in API Reference on [Editor#instance](/docs/api-editor--docs#instance)

useEditor.name: useEditor

tags.comment: when running React.StrictMode the returned editor instance will be undefined on the first call
Each JSON Schema requires a valid JSON Schema type keyword

useEditor.comment:
Create an editor-instance containing a JSON Schema tree, registered widgets and plugins and the
overall state of the data.

interface of generic json editor component.
it takes the current editor and the current node along with a localized option interface

SelectWidgetPlugin.name: SelectWidgetPlugin
SelectWidgetPlugin.comment:
Select Widget will render a JSON Schema `type: "string", enum: [string]` as a selection.

Three select components are available and you can choose between each type using the `x-widget` property:

- per default it will render as normal select input
- `x-widget: "select:taglist` will render a horizontal options represented as tags to be selected directly
- `x-widget: "select:radiogroup` will render a vertical radiogroup with all options visible

properties.name: showHeader
properties.comment: if false, will hide title. will hide complete title-header if no menu-actions are available

properties.name: widgetMenuItems
properties.comment: internal option for menu action items

liveUpdate
properties.comment: if value should update on each keystroke instead of on blur. Defaults to false

properties.name: showHeader
properties.comment: if false, will hide title. will hide complete title-header if no menu-actions are available

properties.name: widgetMenuItems
properties.comment: internal option for menu action items

properties.name: schema
properties.comment: JSON Schema when using JSON Widget on `type: "string"`

properties.name: setup
properties.comment: if value should update on each keystroke instead of on blur. Defaults to false
